concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c concept oceConcept-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
concept.title: 15 The function of the buffer

abstract.p: The Buffer performs the following functions :

li.p: Compensate speed differences between Roll drive and the X-transport drive.
Necessary since the roll drive is continuous and X-transport drive is only driven when required. After each swath.
Correct steering problems in the media feed.
Preheat the media.

abstract.p: The buffer (in red) is located between the Input Unit and X-transport.

section.title: Compensate speed differences between Roll drive and the X-transport drive

li.p: When there is no media in the X-transport pinch yet the buffer is in the home position (outermost position).
This position is detected by the home sensors X motion system buffer right sensor 15B8 XMS_BUF_RIGHT_SE / X motion system buffer left sensor 15B7 XMS_BUF_LEFT_SE.
The media is then fed from the roll drawer to the X-transport pinch .
The media is detected when it moves just past the X-transport pinch by X motion system media sensor 15B11 XMS_MEDIA_SE
The X-transport will transport the media on the platen with a predetermined speed.
When the printing starts both X-transport as well as the roll motor are actively transporting the media.
Speed differences are compensated by the buffer plate.
Normally the buffer is in the middle of its operating range. Here the buffer keeps the same pull on the media that moves towards the X-transport pinch.
The position of the buffer is measured by X motion system buffer left encoder 15B9 XMS_BUF_LEFT_ENC and X motion system buffer right encoder 15B10 XMS_BUF_RIGHT_ENC
If the buffer moves too far inwards the Roll motor will have to speed up. This will feed more media and the buffer will move outwards again.
If the buffer moves too far outwards the Roll motor will have to slow down. This will feed less media and the buffer will move inwards again.
This system will balance the media requirement of the X-transport with the media delivery of the roll motor.

section.title: Correct steering problems in the media feed

li.p: When the media is fed towards the X-transport pinch it is usually not caught on both sides at the same time.
The result is that the media is not fed in, in parallel.
If this is left untreated the media will start to track to the left or right hand side.
The problem is fixed passively by the buffer unit.
On the side where the media caught first the buffer has started moving inward first.
Moving in will created more pull on the X-transport and slow it slightly down.
On the other side the pull on the X-transport is less and media will be fed more easily.
This system will quickly align the media properly again.
You will see that the buffer move back to a parallel position.

section.title: Preheat media
section.p: Some types of supported media buckle very quickly when heated suddenly. This happens on the print surface when the media is suddenly heated to approximately 28 °C. If the media is buckled you can not print on it.
This is the reason that the buffer unit contains a heater set to 32 °C. This will heat the media at a position where buckling is not a problem.

The heaters are X motion system buffer heater 1 15E1XMS_BUF_HE1 / X motion system buffer heater 2 15E2 XMS_BUF_HE2 and the temperature is measured by X motion system buffer temperature sensor 15R1 XMS_BUF_TEMP_SE

section.title: Buffer paper guide
section.p: The buffer is flexible so it can “follow” the media and apply a tension to the media.
The working range of the buffer must be at least 26 mm media length. To manage this the buffer paper guide can rotate 20 degrees.

section.title: Buffer heater
section.p: The media has to be preheated before it reaches the print surface. If the media is not preheated some types of media are likely to wrinkle on the heated print surface. They can and create printhead crashes in extreme cases.
There are 2 buffer heaters (X motion system buffer heater 1 15E1XMS_BUF_HE1 / X motion system buffer heater 2 15E2 XMS_BUF_HE2) which are glued to the inside of the buffer paper guide.
To control the buffer heaters a NTC (X motion system buffer temperature sensor 15R1 XMS_BUF_TEMP_SE) is mounted on the buffer paper guide.
The media is heated to 4 °C above the print surface temperature.

section.title: Static eliminator and grounding
section.p: To remove static on the media a static eliminator is placed at the output of the buffer paper guide.
Because buffer paper guide is hinged isolated with plastic hinges the buffer paper guide is grounded to the frame.

section.title: Buffer control
section.p: To control the position of the buffer paper guide between the end positions 0 and 20 degrees, at both sides a rotation sensor (X motion system buffer left encoder 15B9 XMS_BUF_LEFT_ENC / X motion system buffer right encoder 15B10 XMS_BUF_RIGHT_ENC) is mounted on a wheel.
A cable is mounted on the wheel and adjusted to the buffer outer frames. So the position of the buffer can be measured by both rotation sensors.
To make sure there is no slip between cable and wheel the cable is turned 450 degrees around the wheel.

To know the position of the buffer a home sensor on left (X motion system buffer right sensor 15B8 XMS_BUF_RIGHT_SE) and right (X motion system buffer left sensor 15B7 XMS_BUF_LEFT_SE) side is needed.
This sensor is mounted on the same bracket as the end stop in start position (0 degrees).
To prevent that vane of plate left/right will damage the sensor the bottom plate has a small guide.

section.title: Buffer force
section.p: To get a proper tension on the media there are 4 tension springs.
The 4 springs are connected between the cables mentioned above.
The function of the springs is to provide the force to push the buffer plate outwards.
